data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T000745-2d4e385a/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T000745-2d4e385a/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T000745-2d4e385a/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T000745-2d4e385a/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T000745-2d4e385a/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-6cde5ffd">
    <title>registry: install default package from embedded package FS</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  `ddx init` must install the default `ddx` package offline from embedded package content, using the same installer semantics as `ddx plugin install ddx`. Today the binary embeds a special skill-only tree instead of the default package layout.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/registry/installer.go:20-185 only installs packages by downloading a GitHub tarball. cli/cmd/init.go:276-299 works around offline/default-package needs by separately copying embedded skills and then attempting `registry.InstallPackage(pkg, workingDir)`, which downloads package content and may fail offline. cli/Makefile:65-84 syncs `skills/ddx` into `cli/internal/skills/ddx` for `go:embed`, not a package-rooted `library/` tree. The plan requires `InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, sourceFS, projectRoot)` or equivalent embedded-package support rooted at the default package.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add an embedded default-package FS under the registry package, rooted at the package layout that will be copied from `library/`.
  - Split package install entrypoints into remote, local-dir, and embedded-FS paths that share the same core manifest/mapping implementation.
  - Preserve the existing `InstallPackage(pkg, projectRoot)` public wrapper as the remote path only if callers still need it during the migration.
  - Add offline tests proving an embedded package install can create `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, and `.claude/skills/ddx` without network.

NON-SCOPE
  - Rewriting `ddx init` to call the embedded package installer; a dependent bead owns the init call site.
  - Moving the source skill tree into `library/skills/ddx`; a later bead owns the content move.
  - Removing old embedded skill files before offline init is green.

PARENT + DEPS
  Parent: ddx-6862f2ac. Depends on the registry mapping bead so embedded installs use the same mapping implementation.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `TestInstallPackageFromFSHonorsManifestMappings` proves an embedded `fs.FS` rooted at a package directory reads `package.yaml` and applies root plus skill mappings.
2. `TestInstallPackageFromFSInstallsDefaultDDxPackageOffline` proves the embedded default package can install `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, and `.claude/skills/ddx` without invoking network download code.
3. `TestInstallPackageFromRemoteUsesSharedCore` proves the remote entrypoint and embedded-FS entrypoint share the same skill mapping behavior for a fixture package.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/registry/... -run 'TestInstallPackageFromFSHonorsManifestMappings|TestInstallPackageFromFSInstallsDefaultDDxPackageOffline|TestInstallPackageFromRemoteUsesSharedCore' -count=1` passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/registry/...` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:plugins, area:skills, area:cli, kind:feature, plan-2026-05-13, spec:plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-6862f2ac" spec-id="plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout" base-rev="73e63d63e2a66e74f99dfe2cbce4fe4893dbe255" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260514T000745-2d4e385a"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout" path="docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout.md">DDx Skill Package Layout</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Bead complete. Summary of changes:

- **`cli/internal/registry/installer.go`** — split the install entrypoint into `InstallPackageFromRemote`, `InstallPackageFromDir`, and `InstallPackageFromFS`, all routed through a shared `installFromExtractedDir` core. `InstallPackage` remains as a thin compatibility alias for the remote path. Added `materializeFS` to bridge embed.FS into the existing on-disk install pipeline.
- **`cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/`** — new package exposing `FS()` over `//go:embed all:library`, with a `library/package.yaml` and `library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md` stub so the embedded default package matches the post-migration target layout (`source: skills/`).
- **`cli/internal/registry/installer_test.go`** — added the three required tests: `TestInstallPackageFromFSHonorsManifestMappings`, `TestInstallPackageFromFSInstallsDefaultDDxPackageOffline` (blocks `http.DefaultTransport` to prove offline), and `TestInstallPackageFromRemoteUsesSharedCore`.

ACs verified: registry test suite passes, the named tests all pass, and `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
